home.title: CV
home.portraitAlt: Closeup picture of a woman, Francesca Tynan, smiling.
home.bio1: Hi, I'm Francesca, a Junior Software Engineer at <tmb>TMB Marketing & Communications</tmb>.
home.bio2: A career switcher, I am a <cfg>Code First Girls</cfg> CFGDegree alumna having studied the Software/Data Engineering, Cyber Security & Vulnerability Research pathway, sponsored by <gchq>GCHQ</gchq>.
home.bio3: With my Code First Girls studies delving into security principles and vulnerabilities, secure coding practices are at the forefront of my approach — AAA, the CIA triad, HTTPS, input validation, and security by design. Considerations of access control, salting and hashing, and the principle of least privilege (PoLP) are part of my everyday thinking.
home.bio4: Beyond languages, I'm comfortable with TDD, Git, Jira, Agile, pair programming, and code reviews. Collaborating with colleagues, communicating trade-offs and navigating stakeholder requirements are part of my day-to-day process.
home.bio5: I was also a <cajigo>Cajigo</cajigo> 100 Women in Tech 2024 Mentee.
home.bio6: My previous career background was in customer service, including training a team of new employees, mentoring colleagues and leading a commercial scale smart meter troubleshooting project via Google Sheets and industry-standard diagnostic tooling. I attained awards for performance and produced support materials for the company's intranet knowledge base.

tmb.bullet0: Full stack development across AWS, React, TypeScript, GraphQL, Postgres, Vite, Docker and Node.js.
tmb.bullet1: Consistently working across a range of frontend and backend tickets, building new features across assorted client repositories.

cfg-degree.bullet0: Intensive 16-week CFGDegree bootcamp in Software/Data Engineering.
cfg-degree.bullet1: 4-week '+Masters' Cyber Security extension and 8-week Vulnerability Research specialisation.
cfg-degree.bullet2: Group project: built Code First Guard, a password generator and manager, acting as project manager.
cfg-degree.bullet3: Group Cyber Security Vulnerability Report assessing our own CFGDegree project.
cfg-degree.bullet4: Group Vulnerability Research Report including capture the flags, creating bash and python scripts to automate solutions, manual code review and misc. techniques.

code-first-guard.summary: Customisable password generator and manager built by a team of six for the CFGDegree Summer 2024 programme, delivered within a month.

frantechnia-portfolio.summary: My March 2026 personal portfolio site built with React 19, TypeScript, TanStack Router, and Vite. Featuring i18n, custom colour accent system, and security hardening.

portfolio-v1.summary: December 2023 – March 2026. My first portfolio site was built with vanilla HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. It had a two-column, side navigation layout with video demo.

projects.title: Projects
projects.subtitle: This is a select documentation of projects I have worked on, some of our work is developed for client internal use and isn't possible to share but I continue to develop personal portfolio projects to demonstrate different sets of skills.
